problem_statement: The GovernedPlanningProfile runtime authorization request has been submitted, but a generic continuation input must not be interpreted as signed runtime authorization. The system needs a schema-backed no-effect rejection witness that records generic continuation as non-authorizing while preserving the path for a future explicit signed approval witness.
success_criteria: a deterministic validator emits a generic continuation rejection witness from the current runtime authorization request
the checked-in local rejection witness matches the deterministic produced witness
the schema records GovernanceBlocked and RejectedNoEffect for generic continuation
the witness binds the source runtime authorization request id and hash
the witness records that signed approval remains absent and runtime authorization gate remains unsatisfied

focused tests cover schema compatibility, fixture parity, source request binding, blocked-response evidence, authority-drift rejection, signed-approval drift rejection, unsatisfied source rejection, and boundary drift rejection
non_goals: do not record a signed operator approval witness
do not execute, dispatch, replan, replay, or rollback
do not add a router, worker, scheduler, connector, or runtime registration path
do not claim live runtime activation
do not replace the runtime authorization request
constraints: the rejection witness is local, read-only, deterministic, and reversible
generic continuation is classified as non-authorization
runtime promotion activation remains denied until a separate explicit signed approval and activation action are governed
Mfidel atomicity remains preserved because the validator does not normalize or decompose text
evidence_required: schema-backed local generic continuation rejection fixture
deterministic validator and produced witness parity
SDLC design and security review artifacts
